choices.text: Ask who was around
Ask about the time window
Ask about prior contact
Press the contradiction
Pin down the exact location
Frame it as the victim bringing trouble
Frame it as pressure and circumstance
Frame it as someone trying to protect something
Frame it as something that got out of hand

nodes.text: I keep watch because everybody else on this block minds their curtains. I saw movement near {place}, and {suspect} is the face that keeps floating back up.

choices.text: Ask whether anyone else was there
Ask what made the figure stand out

nodes.text: Could have been somebody else in the spill of shadows, but if there was company they stayed behind the kind of angle neighbors learn not to stare at.
It was the hesitation. People who belong somewhere knock or hurry. This one paused like the building owed them an answer.
It was {time_phrase}. I remember because the pipes had just stopped banging and the street had gone thin enough for little sounds to matter.

choices.text: Ask what happened just before
Ask what happened just after

nodes.text: Before that, the block was ordinary in the sad way it usually is. Then the quiet changed shape around {place}.
Afterward there was a stretch of stillness so deliberate it bothered me more than any shout would have.
Neighborhoods keep a soft ledger. I knew {suspect} the way you know a car that circles too often or a tenant who never waves first.

choices.text: Ask whether they ever spoke
Ask whether the witness trusted them

nodes.text: Only the small civics of living close together. A nod on the stairs. A look that says 'mind your own walls.'
Trust is too expensive for apartment living. I knew how much hallway to give them. That is different.
You want the block to behave like a stopwatch. It behaves like a hallway. Sounds slip, doors muffle, and memory takes whatever shape fits rent and nerves.

choices.text: Tell them the times do not match
Tell them they are withholding the useful part

nodes.text: All right. I rounded the time because I do not enjoy seeing my own name written into ugly nights. That does not make the night clean.
Useful to you is not the same thing as safe to me. Neighbors learn that before they learn each other's birthdays.
You keep saying {place} like the building has one face. It has front steps, side gates, blind corners, and a dozen places where a witness becomes a rumor.

choices.text: Ask for the doorway or side of the building
Ask what the witness could actually see

nodes.text: Closer to the side where tenants cut through when they do not want company. That is the best map I can give you.
Enough to know the movement was wrong for the hour. Not enough to pretend the dark signed an affidavit for me.
Blocks like this collect bad habits and call it atmosphere. By the time trouble shows its face, half the damage has already rented a room upstairs.

choices.text: Ask whether the victim invited the danger
Ask whether the witness is shifting blame

nodes.text: I am saying people keep strange company, prop open bad doors, and act shocked when the building remembers.
Maybe. But if a place keeps warning you with little failures and you ignore them, the final one lands harder.
Pressure leaks through walls before blood does. You hear it in arguments, missed payments, and the way folks stop making eye contact in the stairwell.

choices.text: Ask what pressure was building
Ask whether the witness is excusing it

nodes.text: Money. Shame. People sleeping one thin wall away from decisions they cannot afford. That kind of pressure warps a whole building.
No. I am saying the hallways were already bent before the night finally snapped them.
Everybody in a building like this claims they are covering for somebody. A child. A parent. A lover. Usually they are just trying to survive the mirror.

choices.text: Ask who was supposed to be protected
Ask whether that story sounds convenient

nodes.text: Whoever lives one floor above the consequences. That is the usual answer.
Convenient, sure. Still, people do ugly things under the banner of keeping worse things out of the apartment.
Accidents in close quarters still leave planning in the cleanup. Walls remember where panic stopped and decision started.

choices.text: Ask whether it looked sudden
Ask whether someone tried to fix it after

nodes.text: Sudden, maybe. But not brief. Something in the building stayed tense too long after the first wrong note.
That is the part I believe. Somebody kept moving around after the damage was done, like they thought furniture and silence could rescue them.
